figures.caption: Fig. 1 | Quantum geometry and third-order electrical transport allowed by altermagnetic symmetry in (101)-oriented RuO2 thin films. a, Magnetic crystal structure of altermagnetic RuO2,

Fig. 2 | Third-order electrical transport in a (101)-oriented RuO2 thin film. a, Schematic illustration on the measurement protocol of third-order longitudinal transport. b–d, Relation between third-harmonic longitudinal voltages Vx 3ω and the applied ac current Ix ω measured with increased/decreased current (b), different electrode pairs (c) and frequency (d) at room temperature. Ix ω is directed along [010]. The inset in b displays the data collected with a small Ix ω. The inset in c displays the optical image of a typical Hall bar device. The scale bar represents a length of 5 μm. The electrodes are numbered to indicate which pair is employed in the measurement. e, Schematic illustration on the measurement protocol of the third-order Hall effect. Fig. 3 | Crystalline anisotropy of the third-order electrical transport in a (101)-oriented RuO2 thin film. a, Optical image of the twelve-terminal device. The scale bar represents a length of 10 μm.
